message:
beads: file fcp-host gateway routing review findings (by4vu, 5qp7o, t623k)

Closing the fcp-host invoke_handler / verify_live_request review.
No shipped patches this round — the root fixes all require the host
to carry information it doesn't currently track (connector zones
from manifest, connector instance_id from handshake response).

  by4vu [HIGH]   invoke_handler routes requests purely by
                 connector_id; the capability verifier binds to
                 request.zone_id but never checks that zone_id is
                 in the connector's declared allowed_sources. A
                 valid token for z:secure can invoke a connector
                 whose manifest declares home=z:work, as long as
                 the token's cap_id matches one of the connector's
                 operations. Cross-zone leakage by design oversight.
                 Fix needs RegistryEntry to carry manifest zones.

  5qp7o [MEDIUM] verify_live_request constructs CapabilityVerifier
                 with InstanceId::new() (fresh random UUID per
                 request). The core verifier only checks instance
                 binding if the token declares one — so tokens
                 with `instance_id` are ALWAYS rejected (random
                 UUID can never match), and tokens without
                 `instance_id` pass unchecked. Host captures no
                 instance id from handshake either. Instance
                 binding is architecturally dead at the host.

  t623k [LOW]    invoke_handler passes principal_override=None so
                 the HTTP caller's identity is never cross-checked
                 against the token's subject. Admin endpoints pass
                 a principal header through; the main invoke path
                 does not. By design per the capability model
                 (token IS the principal) but worth a doc note so
                 future readers don't mistake it for an oversight.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

A broader rch exec cargo test -p fcp-testkit --lib compiled and began running, but the mock_server cluster hung once rch failed open to local execution, so full-suite test completion remains unresolved under current infra conditions.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"PinkCitadel","created_at":"2026-03-07T07:57:15.208766Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-07T08:50:22.202683Z","closed_at":"2026-03-07T08:50:22.202664Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":956,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-5e6n8","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"fcp-testkit test expansion complete: harness.rs +27 tests (11→38) with StubConnector mock, mock_server.rs +17 tests (5→22) covering MockApiServer, MockScenarioBuilder, RecordedRequest.  {"id":"flywheel_connectors-5hmfa","title":"Probe Parented Task One","description":"Type: task","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-18T00:53:13.318073Z","updated_at":"2026-03-23T08:00:00.645155Z","closed_at":"2026-03-23T08:00:00.645155Z","close_reason":"Probe task - not actionable.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"flywheel_connectors-5qp7o","title":"[MEDIUM] fcp-host verify_live_request: InstanceId::new() breaks instance-bound tokens + leaves non-bound tokens unchecked","description":"verify_live_request (bin/fcp-host.rs:1272-1276) constructs the CapabilityVerifier with a fresh random InstanceId per request:

    let verifier = CapabilityVerifier::new(
        capability_key.to_bytes(),
        request.zone_id.clone(),
        InstanceId::new(),  // fresh Uuid::new_v4() every call
    );

The verifier's instance-binding check (fcp-core/src/capability.rs:1505-1520) says:

    if let Some(inst_val) = claims.get(fcp2_claims::INSTANCE_ID) {
        let inst_str = inst_val.as_text()...?;
        if inst_str != self.instance_id.as_str() {
            return Err(FcpError::ZoneViolation { ... });
        }
    }
    // else: no instance check at all

So with a per-request random instance_id:
  (a) Any token that DOES declare `instance_id` is ALWAYS rejected — the caller's instance claim can never match a UUID the host just invented. Instance-bound tokens are effectively banned from the host.
  (b) Any token that DOES NOT declare `instance_id` passes without any instance check. Instance binding is silently opt-in for the token issuer and un-enforced by the host.

The host has no link from a running SubprocessConnector to its instance_id either — handshake uses `requested_instance_id: None` (bin/fcp-host.rs:220) and the connector chooses its own id, which the host never captures.

Net: instance binding is architecturally dead at the host gateway. Either:
  1. Honest: drop the instance check at the host entirely (pass an Option<InstanceId> to CapabilityVerifier and skip the check when None), OR
  2. Enforce: capture instance_id from handshake response into RegistryEntry and pass the real id so instance-bound tokens verify correctly against the connector they're addressed to.

Option 1 is the smaller change and matches current intent (host enforces zone+capability, not instance). Option 2 is the stronger security story (instance hand-off survives reissue/race).

Files: crates/fcp-host/src/bin/fcp-host.rs:1272-1276, crates/fcp-core/src/capability.rs:1505-1520.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T08:36:50.374308Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T08:36:50.374308Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-5szl","title":"TEST: Agent Integration Unit Tests (Introspection, Tool Descriptors, Discovery)","description":"# TEST: Agent Integration Unit Tests

## Goal
Verify that FCP2 operations are discoverable and safe for AI agents.

## Test Categories

### 1. Introspection Schema Tests
- Introspection object schema validation (canonical CBOR)
- All required fields present (operations, schemas, risk levels)
- Golden vectors for introspection responses

### 2. Operation Discovery Tests
- Enumerate all connector operations
- Each operation has input/output schemas
- Risk tier correctly reported
- Rate limits correctly reported

### 3. MCP Tool Descriptor Tests
- Mapping to MCP-compatible tool descriptors
- Tool name, description, examples present
- Input/output schema translation
- Risk tier to MCP safety level mapping

### 4. E2E Discovery Tests
- Agent can enumerate tools from connector
- Agent can invoke safe operation end-to-end
- Agent can read recovery hints on failure

## Logging Requirements
- Structured JSON with operation_id, schema versions
- Discovery timing metrics
- Error details with recovery hints

## Acceptance Criteria
- Introspection is complete for agent operation
- No hardcoded connector knowledge required
- Tool descriptors match MCP conventions","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-15T08:50:32.192813258Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-28T20:59:34.312640984Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T20:59:34.312513066Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["agent","fcp2","mvp","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-5szl","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.30","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-5szl","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.35","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-5un1","title":"TEST: fcp-telemetry Unit Test Suite","description":"# TEST: fcp-telemetry Unit Test Suite


## Goal
Validate `fcp-telemetry` emits spec-required metrics/logging fields, preserves correlation, and never leaks secrets.

## Scope
Comprehensive unit tests for metrics, logging, and tracing library.

## Test Categories

### Structured Logging
- [ ] Log level filtering
- [ ] JSON output format
- [ ] Field injection (correlation IDs, zone, connector)
- [ ] Sensitive data redaction
- [ ] Log rotation handling

### Metrics Collection
- [ ] Counter increments
- [ ] Gauge values
- [ ] Histogram distributions
- [ ] Timing measurements
- [ ] Label/tag handling

### Distributed Tracing
- [ ] Span creation and nesting
- [ ] Context propagation
- [ ] Trace ID generation
- [ ] Span attributes
- [ ] Sampling decisions

### Export
- [ ] Prometheus format export
- [ ] OTLP format export
- [ ] Custom sink support
- [ ] Batch flushing

### Performance
- [ ] Low overhead in hot path
- [ ] Async export
- [ ] Buffer management

## Coverage Target: >90%

## Acceptance Criteria
- Runs deterministically in CI (no flake due to timing/network).
- Uses mocks only (no real external network).
- Emits structured JSON logs per `flywheel_connectors-1n78.35` with stable `test_name` identifiers.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-5vy","title":"fcp.webhook-receiver: Inbound Event Gateway","description":"# Inbound Event Gateway Meta-Connector

**Force Multiplier:** Universal webhook receiver for external service integrations.

## Overview

Many services push events via webhooks (GitHub, Stripe, Discord, Slack, etc.). Instead of building separate webhook handlers for each service, this meta-connector provides a unified HTTP endpoint infrastructure with per-service configuration.

## Core Components

### 1. HTTP Endpoint Management
- Dynamic endpoint creation/deletion
- Unique URL per webhook source
- Support for path-based routing (`/webhooks/{service}/{id}`)
- Health check endpoints
- CORS and preflight handling
- Rate limiting per endpoint

### 2. Signature Verification (Per-Service)
- Pluggable verification strategies:
  - HMAC-SHA256 (GitHub, Stripe)
  - HMAC-SHA1 (legacy services)
  - Ed25519 (Discord)
  - API key header
  - Basic auth
  - Custom verification functions
- Timing-safe comparison
- Signature caching for replay prevention
- Failed verification alerting

### 3. Payload Transformation
- JSON schema validation
- Payload normalization to common event format
- Field mapping/extraction
- Nested payload unwrapping
- Binary payload handling (multipart)
- Compression support (gzip, deflate)

### 4. Event Routing to Zones
- Route events to appropriate FCP zones
- Support multiple destinations per event type
- Filter events based on payload content
- Priority-based routing
- Fan-out to multiple handlers

### 5. Retry/Dead-Letter Handling
- Configurable retry strategies:
  - Exponential backoff
  - Fixed interval
  - Fibonacci backoff
- Dead letter queue for failed events
- Manual retry interface
- Event replay capability
- TTL-based expiration

## Configuration Example

```toml
[[webhooks]]
name = "github-repo-events"
path = "/webhooks/github/main-repo"
signature_type = "hmac-sha256"
signature_header = "X-Hub-Signature-256"
secret = "${GITHUB_WEBHOOK_SECRET}"
route_to = "zone.github-automation"
event_type_header = "X-GitHub-Event"

[[webhooks]]
name = "stripe-payments"
path = "/webhooks/stripe/payments"
signature_type = "stripe"
secret = "${STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET}"
route_to = "zone.billing"
retry_max = 5
retry_backoff = "exponential"

[[webhooks]]
name = "discord-interactions"
path = "/webhooks/discord/bot"
signature_type = "ed25519"
public_key = "${DISCORD_PUBLIC_KEY}"
route_to = "zone.discord-bot"
```

## Event Format (Normalized)

```json
{
  "id": "evt_abc123",
  "source": "github",
  "type": "push",
  "timestamp": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z",
  "webhook_id": "github-repo-events",
  "payload": { ... },
  "metadata": {
    "delivery_id": "xyz789",
    "signature_valid": true,
    "received_at": "2024-01-15T10:30:00.123Z"
  }
}
```

## Success Criteria

- [ ] Support 10+ common webhook signature types
- [ ] Sub-50ms processing latency
- [ ] 99.9% uptime for webhook endpoints
- [ ] Complete audit trail for all received webhooks
- [ ] Zero data loss with dead-letter queue
- [ ] Hot-reload of webhook configurations

## Dependencies

- HTTP server framework (axum/actix-web)
- Persistent queue for dead-letter handling
- Zone routing system

## Security Considerations

- All secrets stored encrypted
- IP allowlisting per webhook (optional)
- Request size limits
- Timeout handling
- DDoS protection integration","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-11T15:23:06.647234418Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T15:53:35.455928212Z","closed_at":"2026-01-11T15:53:35.455928212Z","close_reason":"Duplicate of flywheel_connectors-3md (fcp.webhook-receiver: Inbound Event Gateway Connector)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-bs5ih.9.2","title":"[FCP3-V3.8.2] E2E tests: connector lifecycle with RetryLoop under wiremock","description":"## Goal
E2E integration tests exercising full connector lifecycle with V3 runtime patterns.

## Test list (4 tests minimum)
1. `e2e_deadline_enforcement` — configure connector → invoke with short deadline → wiremock delays → verify timeout error (not hang)
2. `e2e_retry_on_429` — configure → invoke → wiremock returns 429 first, 200 second → verify success after retry
3. `e2e_retry_exhaustion_529` — configure → wiremock always returns 529 → verify retries exhaust and return overloaded error
4. `e2e_graceful_shutdown` — configure → start invoke → shutdown() → verify in-flight cancelled

## Implementation details
- Use anthropic connector (already has full V3 migration) as test subject
- Use wiremock MockServer for HTTP mocking
- Use short timeouts (100ms-1s) to keep tests fast
- Each test emits structured JSON logs

## Logging contract
Each test must verify log output contains:
- `connector_id`, `operation_id`, `zone_id`
- For retry tests: `attempt`, `delay_ms`, `error`
- For timeout: `timeout_ms`, `error_type="deadline_timeout"`

## Acceptance criteria
- All 4 tests pass deterministically
- Tests complete in < 10s total (no 60s retry waits)
- Log output is machine-parseable JSON
- Place in connectors/anthropic/tests/v3_lifecycle.rs or crates/fcp-e2e/tests/v3_lifecycle_e2e.rs","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T22:01:13.391584Z","created_by":"BeigeCave","updated_at":"2026-03-15T00:38:55.299981Z","closed_at":"2026-03-15T00:38:55.299546Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","fcp3","logging","testing","v3"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-bs5ih.9.2","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-bs5ih.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T22:01:13.391584Z","created_by":"BeigeCave","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-bs5ih.9.2","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-bs5ih.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T22:01:13.391584Z","created_by":"BeigeCave","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1642,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-bs5ih.9.2","author":"jemanuel","text":"PlumCrane: Created 4 E2E lifecycle tests in connectors/anthropic/tests/v3_lifecycle.rs. All pass in 0.11s: (1) e2e_retry_on_429 - retries 502 then succeeds (2) e2e_retry_exhaustion - exhausts retries on persistent 502 (3) e2e_deadline_enforcement - runtime deadline fires in ~100ms instead of hanging (4) e2e_graceful_shutdown - shutdown propagates to all contexts.","created_at":"2026-03-15T00:38:48Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-bs5ih.9.3","title":"[FCP3-V3.8.3] Structured logging verification: tracing span contract for V3 operations","description":"## Goal
Verify the structured tracing contract for V3 operations.

## Tracing span contract
Every V3 invoke must produce spans containing:
1. `connector_id` (string) — which connector
2. `operation_id` (string) — which operation
3. `duration_ms` (u64) — wall clock time
4. `result` — "success" or "error"
5. For errors: `fcp_error_code` (string)
6. For retries: `attempt` (u32), `delay_ms` (u64), `error` (string)

## Implementation
Use `tracing-subscriber` with a test Layer that captures spans:
```rust
struct TestCapture { spans: Arc<Mutex<Vec<CapturedSpan>>> }
impl<S: Subscriber> Layer<S> for TestCapture { ... }
```

Run connector operations and assert captured spans match the contract.

## Acceptance criteria
- Test verifies all 6 span fields are present for success path
- Test verifies retry spans include attempt/delay_ms/error
- Test verifies error spans include fcp_error_code
- Place in crates/fcp-sdk/tests/tracing_contract.rs

## Testing
- Deterministic: use mock connector, not real HTTP
- Fast: no network calls, no retries needed for success path test","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T22:01:13.391584Z","created_by":"BeigeCave","updated_at":"2026-03-15T15:50:08.076456Z","closed_at":"2026-03-15T15:50:08.075430Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fcp3","logging","testing","tracing","v3"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-bs5ih.9.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-bs5ih.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T22:01:13.391584Z","created_by":"BeigeCave","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-bs5ih.9.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-bs5ih.9.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T22:01:13.391584Z","created_by":"BeigeCave","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1650,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-bs5ih.9.3","author":"jemanuel","text":"PlumCrane: RetryLoop (migration.rs:476-481) already emits structured tracing with attempt, delay_ms, error fields via warn!() macro. Connector invoke paths emit connector_id, operation_id, duration_ms via #[instrument] spans. E2E behavioral coverage exists in v3_lifecycle.rs. Formal tracing-subscriber Layer capture tests deferred — the contract is enforced by RetryLoop infrastructure.","created_at":"2026-03-15T03:36:06Z"},{"id":1655,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-bs5ih.9.3","author":"jemanuel","text":"MistyPond: Implemented tracing_contract.rs with 9 tests covering all V3 structured logging paths: success path (attempt field), retry path (attempt/delay_ms/error fields), terminal error (no retry warnings), max attempts exhausted (correct retry count), explicit retry_after (delay matches hint), cancellation (no events), runtime lifecycle integration, multiple retries with incrementing attempts and exponential backoff, and debug event count verification. All tests pass, clippy clean, fmt clean. Uses custom tracing-subscriber Layer to capture events.","created_at":"2026-03-15T15:48:16Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-bvs","title":"Study: Clawdbot Connector Patterns - Synthesis & Recommendations","description":"# Study: Clawdbot Connector Patterns - Synthesis & Recommendations


## Goal
Study and synthesize Clawdbot connector patterns into actionable guidance for FCP2 connector design (security, state, tests).

## Objective

Synthesize findings from Discord and Telegram connector studies into actionable patterns and recommendations for FCP connector development.

## Synthesis Areas

### 1. Common Patterns Identified
- [ ] Shared architectural patterns
- [ ] Common abstractions
- [ ] Reusable utility functions
- [ ] Configuration patterns
- [ ] Error handling approaches

### 2. Platform-Specific Adaptations
- [ ] How patterns adapt to different APIs
- [ ] Platform-specific optimizations
- [ ] Unique challenges per platform
- [ ] Platform capability differences

### 3. Reusable Components Catalog

#### Core Infrastructure
- Connection management
- Retry/backoff utilities
- Rate limiter
- Event dispatcher
- Configuration loader

#### Message Processing
- Command parser
- Response builder
- Attachment handler
- Formatter utilities

#### State Management
- Session manager
- Cache layer
- Persistence adapter

### 4. Anti-Patterns to Avoid
- [ ] Identified code smells
- [ ] Scalability issues
- [ ] Security concerns
- [ ] Maintainability problems
- [ ] Testing difficulties

### 5. FCP Connector Design Recommendations

#### Architecture
- Recommended module structure
- Interface definitions
- Dependency injection patterns
- Plugin system design

#### Implementation Guidelines
- Coding standards
- Error handling strategy
- Logging requirements
- Testing requirements

#### Documentation Standards
- API documentation
- Configuration documentation
- Troubleshooting guides

## Deliverables

1. **FCP Connector Template** - Skeleton project with best practices
2. **Pattern Library** - Reusable patterns with examples
3. **Style Guide** - Coding and architecture standards
4. **Checklist** - Connector review checklist
5. **Migration Guide** - How to port existing connectors to FCP

## Success Criteria

- Clear, actionable recommendations
- Reusable code templates
- Documented patterns with rationale
- Identified gaps requiring new solutions
- Roadmap for connector development

## Dependencies

- Completion of Discord connector study
- Completion of Telegram connector study

## Time Estimate

- Synthesis: 3-4 hours
- Template creation: 2-3 hours
- Documentation: 2-3 hours
- Total: 7-10 hours

## Acceptance Criteria
- Runs deterministically in CI (no flake due to timing/network).
- Uses mocks only (no real external network).
- Emits structured JSON logs per `flywheel_connectors-1n78.35` with stable `test_name` identifiers.
","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CopperBrook","created_at":"2026-01-11T15:24:02.200654302Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-07T05:33:13.975867Z","closed_at":"2026-03-07T05:33:13.975849Z","close_reason":"Completed synthesis into docs/fcp_model_connectors_rust.md","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-bvs","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-v6w","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-bvs","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-w1g","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"flywheel_connectors-by4vu","title":"[HIGH] fcp-host invoke_handler does not enforce connector home-zone binding — cross-zone invocation","description":"Request routing in the host gateway never consults the connector's declared `zones.home` / `zones.allowed_sources` from its manifest. A valid capability token for zone Z lets any holder invoke *any* connector whose operations match the token's `capability` claim — regardless of which zone the connector was declared to serve.

Trace:
  1. POST /invoke -> invoke_handler (bin/fcp-host.rs:3782)
     - Looks up connector by request.connector_id only.
     - Calls evaluate_live_preflight(state, request, None).
  2. evaluate_live_preflight (bin/fcp-host.rs:1375)
     - Runs state.discovery.preflight() which, with BudgetPolicyEngine (budget.rs:364), only enforces *budget*. No zone-vs-connector check.
     - Runs verify_live_request (bin/fcp-host.rs:1249).
  3. verify_live_request:
     - CapabilityVerifier::new(capability_key, request.zone_id, InstanceId::new())  -- verifier is bound to the request's zone, not the connector's.
     - verify_token against tool.capability.
     - No check that request.zone_id ∈ connector.manifest.zones.allowed_sources.
  4. state.registry.invoke (SubprocessRegistry::invoke, bin/fcp-host.rs:516):
     - Looks up connector by id, forwards.
     - SubprocessConnector::invoke ensures handshake for request.zone_id; if the connector can handshake any zone on demand, no connector-side enforcement either.

Concrete exploit sketch:
  - Connector A is declared `home = z:work`, exposes capability `airtable.read`.
  - Attacker obtains a valid token signed by the host key with `iss_zone = z:secure`, `cap_id = airtable.read`.
  - Attacker sends POST /invoke with connector_id = A, zone_id = z:secure, capability_token = that token.
  - Preflight: budget ok. verify_live_request binds verifier to z:secure; token zone z:secure matches; token cap airtable.read matches A's tool.capability. Invoke proceeds.
  - Result: a z:work-only connector executes a z:secure-context request.

Root cause: ManagedConnectorConfig (admin_state.rs:33) carries no zones; the host never parses the connector's manifest.toml [zones] section. The host has no way to enforce "this connector may only be invoked from these zones."

Fix shape:
  - Parse connector manifest at load time, store zones.home + zones.allowed_sources in RegistryEntry alongside config.
  - In verify_live_request (or a new `check_zone_binding` step), reject if request.zone_id is not in connector.allowed_sources.
  - Emit a distinct ZoneMismatch error code (FCP-3xxx) so callers see it in receipts.
  - Add an integration test: connector with home=z:work must reject zone_id=z:secure even with a valid z:secure-scoped token.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T08:36:34.855284Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T08:36:34.855284Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-bz431","title":"[FCP3/P5.5] Add mesh conformance and end to end coverage for placement and failover","description":"Background: once a pilot works, the mesh specific guarantees need automated regression coverage. Placement and failover are exactly the sort of system behaviors that can silently regress under unrelated changes.

Scope: add conformance and end to end coverage for placement decisions, lease and fencing behavior, failover, checkpoint resume, split brain prevention, and hostile recovery cases. Include scripted fault injection and soak style scenarios with detailed cross node logging, timeline markers, and artifact capture so regressions are diagnosable rather than merely reproducible.

Acceptance: mesh placement and failover guarantees are protected by comprehensive automated tests, end to end scripts, and rich logs rather than by one successful demo run.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:07.775082Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","updated_at":"2026-04-02T21:54:11.375434Z","closed_at":"2026-04-02T21:54:11.375223Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","fcp3","fcp3-refoundation","logging","mesh-first","phase-5","soak","tests"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-bz431","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-0qtvu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:44.454030Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2291,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-bz431","author":"jemanuel","text":"SunnyMoose 2026-04-02: Claiming.  {"id":"flywheel_connectors-bza06","title":"[airtable] Exit protocol loop after shutdown acknowledgment","description":"connectors/airtable/src/main.rs routes the `shutdown` RPC to AirtableConnector::handle_shutdown(), which returns `{"status":"shutdown"}`, but the outer stdin loop keeps reading lines forever. This violates the FCP shutdown contract and can leave the host waiting on a connector that already acknowledged shutdown. Make the protocol handler surface a terminal flag and break the loop after writing the shutdown response, with unit coverage for both response contents and exit behavior.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:46:20.071541Z","created_by":"ChartreuseCardinal","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:58:11.915861Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:58:11.915705Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-t11","title":"fcp.arxiv: Author information and publication history","description":"# fcp.arxiv: Author information and publication history (FCP2)

## Goal
Provide author-centric discovery:
- list papers by author name
- fetch author publication history (bounded)
- optional: infer topics/categories distribution

## Scope
- Search papers by author filter.
- Aggregate author stats:
  - papers per category
  - publication timeline
- Optional: disambiguation hints (best-effort).

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `arxiv.author.read`

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- NetworkConstraints pinned.
- Output bounded:
  - max papers returned
  - stable ordering

## Deliverables
- Operation schemas:
  - `arxiv.author.papers.list`
  - `arxiv.author.stats`

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Mock-only tests for:
  - author query encoding
  - bounds enforcement

## Acceptance criteria
- Author history queries are reliable, bounded, and deterministic.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-t1hy0","title":"[FCP3/P7] Cut over, delete transition scaffolding, and prove the architecture","description":"Background: early transition architectures often accumulate a permanent layer of temporary shims, compatibility buckets, and explanatory caveats. That would defeat the point of the V3 refoundation.

Scope: maintain explicit exit gates, delete host first only scaffolding once replacements are proven, retire the broad semantic bucket patterns, and close only on proof. Phase 7 must culminate in reproducible unit tests, integration tests, end to end test scripts, failover drills, performance evidence, preserved transcripts, benchmark baselines, and detailed structured logs showing that the final system is both architecturally cleaner and operationally more trustworthy for users.

Acceptance: the project no longer needs a separate narrative to explain why the real implementation differs from the intended architecture, and the final state is backed by explicit deletion proof, verification artifacts, tests, logs, and user visible workflow preservation.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:02.895006Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","updated_at":"2026-04-19T16:13:01.643870Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T16:13:01.643251Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cutover","fcp3","fcp3-refoundation","mesh-first","phase-7","proof"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-t1hy0","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-0aczd","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:16.836603Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-t1hy0","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-84phy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:17.056051Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-t1hy0","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-8bqme","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:16.625311Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-t1hy0","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-etp8q","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:16.160201Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-t1hy0","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-ukr33","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T20:30:14.813412Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-t1hy0","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-z1nkz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:16.394992Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2054,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-t1hy0","author":"CobaltHarbor","text":"Anything +{"id":"flywheel_connectors-t623k","title":"[LOW] fcp-host invoke_handler passes principal_override=None — HTTP caller identity never bound to token principal","description":"The HTTP invoke endpoint (bin/fcp-host.rs:3813) calls:

    let preflight = evaluate_live_preflight(&state, &request, None).await;

That `None` for principal_override means the HTTP caller is never asked "who are you?" — the principal is whatever the capability token says it is. Admin endpoints (preflight_handler at line 3522, simulate_handler at line 3601) DO pass a principal header through for cross-check; the primary invoke endpoint does not.

Is this impersonation? Only under a specific threat model:
  - If the host's capability-signing key mints tokens for multiple principals, any client that holds *any* valid token can invoke as *any* of those principals by producing the right token. Capability-based security treats the token as the identity, so this is by design — but it means the HTTP layer cannot add defense-in-depth (e.g. mTLS client cert -> principal mapping) on top.
  - If the threat model includes "token stolen from principal A, used to invoke as A by attacker", the HTTP endpoint has no way to detect the mismatch.

This is intentional per the capability model, but worth a doc note on invoke_handler so future readers don't mistake the `None` for an oversight.

Suggested remediation (defensive, not required):
  - Optional X-Principal header wired through to principal_override on the HTTP invoke endpoint, rejected on mismatch with token subject. Admin API already takes this shape.
  - Explicit #[doc] on invoke_handler stating the "token is the principal" model.

Low priority. Files: crates/fcp-host/src/bin/fcp-host.rs:3782-3889.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-20T08:37:04.549792Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T08:37:04.549792Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-t690","title":"TEST: fcp-host Unit Test Suite","description":"# TEST: fcp-host Unit Test Suite

## Goal
Validate `fcp-host` core behaviors (supervision, sandboxing, policy enforcement, logs) with deterministic tests and mocks.

## Scope
Unit tests for the Gateway/Orchestrator implementation.

## Test Categories

### Connector Supervisor
- [ ] Connector process spawning
- [ ] Process termination handling
- [ ] Health monitoring (heartbeat success/failure)
- [ ] Restart policy enforcement (max_restarts, backoff)
- [ ] Resource limit enforcement (memory, CPU)
- [ ] Crash detection and recovery
- [ ] Graceful shutdown signaling

#### Golden Vectors
```rust
// tests/vectors/host/supervisor_restart_policy.json
{
  "policy": {
    "max_restarts": 3,
    "backoff_base_ms": 100,
    "backoff_max_ms": 10000,
    "window_seconds": 60
  },
  "crashes": [
    {"at_ms": 0, "restart": true, "delay_ms": 100},
    {"at_ms": 200, "restart": true, "delay_ms": 200},
    {"at_ms": 600, "restart": true, "delay_ms": 400},
    {"at_ms": 1500, "restart": false, "reason": "max_restarts_exceeded"}
  ]
}
```

### IPC Hub
- [ ] Message routing correctness
- [ ] Request/response correlation
- [ ] Timeout handling (configurable per operation)
- [ ] Event fan-out to multiple subscribers
- [ ] Connection lifecycle (connect, disconnect, reconnect)
- [ ] Backpressure handling

#### IPC Test Scenarios
```rust
#[test]
fn test_request_response_correlation() {
    // Concurrent requests must be correlated correctly
    let hub = IpcHub::new();
    let req1 = hub.send_request("connector-a", Request { id: 1, ... }).await;
    let req2 = hub.send_request("connector-a", Request { id: 2, ... }).await;
    
    // Responses can arrive out of order
    hub.receive_response(Response { id: 2, ... });
    hub.receive_response(Response { id: 1, ... });
    
    // Each request gets correct response
    assert_eq\!(req1.await?.id, 1);
    assert_eq\!(req2.await?.id, 2);
}

#[test]
fn test_timeout_handling() {
    let hub = IpcHub::new_with_timeout(Duration::from_millis(100));
    let req = hub.send_request("connector-a", Request { ... });
    
    // No response within timeout
    let err = req.await.unwrap_err();
    assert\!(matches\!(err, IpcError::Timeout { operation_id, .. }));
}
```

### Zone Enforcer
- [ ] Zone configuration loading from ZoneDefinition
- [ ] Request zone validation (single-zone binding)
- [ ] Cross-zone policy enforcement (default deny)
- [ ] Data flow control (taint labels)
- [ ] Zone membership verification

### Capability Verifier
- [ ] COSE_Sign1 token parsing
- [ ] Ed25519 signature verification
- [ ] Capability scope checking (operation, connector)
- [ ] Constraint enforcement (rate limits, resource limits)
- [ ] Expiration handling (valid_from, valid_until)
- [ ] Grant object ID subset verification
- [ ] Revocation check integration

#### Capability Test Scenarios
```rust
// tests/vectors/host/capability_verification.json
[
  {
    "name": "valid_token",
    "token_hex": "d28443...",
    "expected": {"valid": true, "operations": ["invoke"]}
  },
  {
    "name": "expired_token",
    "token_hex": "d28443...",
    "expected": {"valid": false, "error": "FCP-2102"}
  },
  {
    "name": "invalid_signature",
    "token_hex": "d28443...",
    "expected": {"valid": false, "error": "FCP-2104"}
  },
  {
    "name": "revoked_grant",
    "token_hex": "d28443...",
    "revocation_seq": 42,
    "expected": {"valid": false, "error": "FCP-2105"}
  }
]
```

### Sandbox Manager
- [ ] Filesystem isolation setup (allowed paths, read-only mounts)
- [ ] Network filtering rules (NetworkConstraints)
- [ ] Configuration generation (TOML/JSON for jail)
- [ ] Resource cgroup configuration
- [ ] Credential injection (sealed secrets)

#### Sandbox Test Scenarios
```rust
#[test]
fn test_filesystem_isolation() {
    let sandbox = SandboxManager::new();
    let config = sandbox.configure(&ConnectorManifest {
        network_constraints: NetworkConstraints::Moderate,
        filesystem_access: vec\!["/data/connector"],
        ...
    });
    
    assert\!(config.allowed_paths.contains("/data/connector"));
    assert\!(\!config.allowed_paths.contains("/etc/passwd"));
    assert\!(config.read_only_paths.contains("/usr"));
}

#[test]
fn test_network_filtering_strict() {
    let config = sandbox.configure(&ConnectorManifest {
        network_constraints: NetworkConstraints::Strict,
        ...
    });
    
    // Strict = no raw sockets, DNS only to resolver
    assert\!(\!config.allow_raw_sockets);
    assert_eq\!(config.dns_servers, vec\!["127.0.0.53"]);
}
```

### Agent API
- [ ] HTTP endpoint routing (REST operations)
- [ ] WebSocket handling (streaming subscriptions)
- [ ] Request validation (schema verification)
- [ ] Response formatting (canonical JSON)
- [ ] Error handling (structured FCP errors)
- [ ] Rate limiting (per-principal)

### Connector Registry
- [ ] Manifest parsing (TOML, embedded)
- [ ] Signature verification (Ed25519 over manifest hash)
- [ ] Version comparison (semver)
- [ ] Discovery (local, registry)
- [ ] Content-addressed storage (blake3 hash)

## Mocking Strategy
- Mock connector processes (no real subprocess)
- Mock IPC communications (channel-based)
- Inject various failure modes (crash, timeout, malformed)
- MockClock for deterministic timeout testing

## Test File Organization
```
crates/fcp-host/tests/
├── supervisor_tests.rs
├── ipc_tests.rs
├── zone_enforcer_tests.rs
├── capability_tests.rs
├── sandbox_tests.rs
├── agent_api_tests.rs
├── registry_tests.rs
└── vectors/
    └── host/
        ├── supervisor_restart_policy.json
        ├── capability_verification.json
        ├── sandbox_configs.json
        └── ipc_scenarios.json
```

## Logging Requirements
All tests MUST emit structured JSON:
```json
{
  "timestamp": "RFC3339",
  "test_name": "test_supervisor_restart_policy",
  "module": "fcp-host",
  "phase": "execute|assert|cleanup",
  "correlation_id": "uuid",
  "component": "supervisor|ipc|zone|capability|sandbox|api|registry",
  "operation": "spawn|terminate|verify|configure",
  "connector_id": "test-connector",
  "result": "pass|fail",
  "duration_ms": 5
}
```

### For Capability Tests
```json
{
  "token_id": "...",
  "validation_result": "valid|invalid",
  "error_code": "FCP-2102",
  "operations_allowed": ["invoke"],
  "constraints_enforced": true
}
```

### For Supervisor Tests
```json
{
  "connector_id": "...",
  "event": "spawn|crash|restart|terminate",
  "restart_count": 2,
  "restart_delay_ms": 200,
  "reason": "health_check_failed"
}
```

## CI Integration
```bash
cargo test -p fcp-host -- --nocapture --test-threads=4
```

## Coverage Target: >90%

## Acceptance Criteria
- Runs deterministically in CI (no flake due to timing/network)
- Uses mocks only (no real external network or subprocesses)
- Emits structured JSON logs per `flywheel_connectors-1n78.35` with stable `test_name` identifiers
- All test scenarios have corresponding golden vectors

Verified with cargo test -p fcp-host fcp_host_binary_exposes_discovery_routes -- --nocapture, cargo test -p fcp-host fcp_host_binary_emits_structured_endpoint_logs -- --nocapture, cargo clippy -p fcp-host --all-targets -- -D warnings, and cargo fmt -p fcp-host --check. Agent Mail MCP transport remained unavailable at http://127.0.0.1:8765/api/, so I could not send the completion note there.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SandyStream","created_at":"2026-01-11T17:31:49.774217315Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-17T02:02:58.851157Z","closed_at":"2026-03-17T02:02:58.850290Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fcp2","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-t690","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.35","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-t690","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-oip0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1706,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-t690","author":"jemanuel","text":"MistyPond: Verified acceptance criteria:

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-t77","title":"fcp.jira: Comment and attachment handling","description":"# fcp.jira: Comment and attachment handling (FCP2)

## Goal
Add first-class support for Jira issue **comments** and **attachments**, including safe upload/download flows that preserve FCP2 security invariants.

Comments and attachments are high-leverage for real workflows, but they also carry:
- PII risk
- large binary payload risk
- data exfil risk

## Scope
### Comments
- List comments for an issue (pagination).
- Create/update/delete comments (with visibility rules when supported).
- Convert between:
  - Jira Cloud ADF (Atlassian Document Format)
  - a canonical connector representation (e.g., markdown + structured spans)

### Attachments
- List attachments for an issue (metadata only).
- Download attachment content (streamed; size limits; hash).
- Upload attachment (streamed; content-type allowlist; filename sanitization).
- Delete attachment (dangerous; approval-gated).

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `jira.comments.read`
- `jira.comments.write`
- `jira.attachments.read`
- `jira.attachments.write` (dangerous: data egress into Jira)
- `jira.attachments.delete` (dangerous)

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- NetworkConstraints pinned to Jira base hostname and any attachment host if distinct.
- No secrets in logs.
- **Binary safety**:
  - enforce max download size; abort early
  - enforce max upload size; abort early
  - compute content hash for receipts/audit evidence
- **Taint model**:
  - comment bodies and attachment filenames are tainted inputs
  - do not allow tainted text to trigger dangerous operations without approval

## Deliverables
- Operation schemas:
  - `jira.comment.list`
  - `jira.comment.create`
  - `jira.comment.update`
  - `jira.comment.delete`
  - `jira.attachment.list`
  - `jira.attachment.download`
  - `jira.attachment.upload`
  - `jira.attachment.delete`
- Streaming download/upload helpers compatible with sandboxed IO.

## Test strategy (MUST)
### Unit/integration (mock-only)
- ADF ↔ markdown conversion golden tests.
- Upload tests:
  - filename normalization
  - content-type allowlist
  - size limits
- Download tests:
  - streaming behavior
  - early abort on oversized content
  - hash correctness

### Compliance
- Any write/delete emits OperationReceipt + AuditEvent with:
  - issue key
  - comment id / attachment id
  - content hash (never raw content)

## Acceptance criteria
- Comments round-trip through conversion without losing critical structure.
- Attachment IO is streaming + bounded (no unbounded buffering).
- Dangerous operations are capability + (policy) approval gated and audited.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-t9nj","title":"FIX: connector_target_from_env_matches_runtime test arch normalization","description":"## Bug Summary

The test `connector_target_from_env_matches_runtime` was failing because it compared `ConnectorTarget::from_env().arch` directly with `std::env::consts::ARCH`.

However, `ConnectorTarget::from_env()` intentionally normalizes architecture names for OCI/Docker compatibility:
- `x86_64` → `amd64`
- `aarch64` → `arm64`

## Fix

Updated the test to expect the normalized arch value by using the same mapping logic that `from_env()` uses.

## Files Changed

- `crates/fcp-registry/src/lib.rs`: Fixed test assertion to account for arch normalization

## Verification

- All 48 fcp-registry tests pass
- Full workspace tests pass
- No clippy warnings introduced","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T06:29:43.400900968Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T06:29:59.155461943Z","closed_at":"2026-01-17T06:29:59.155461943Z","close_reason":"Closed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["registry","testing"]}
